name: Chocolate Bread Pudding
recipeInstructions:
Pudding: Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Grease a 9 by 12 inch cake pan. Cut the baguette into 1/4 inch slices and brush with melted butter. Broil for a few seconds and set aside. Melt 1 cup chocolate morsels in the top of a double boiler and set aside. Heat cream and milk until warm. Do not scald. Beat whole eggs, egg yolks, sugar, and vanilla together. Slowly pour the warm cream and milk over the egg mixture and blend. Pour the mixture into chocolate and whisk until no lumps of sugar remain. Line bread slices, buttered side up, in the pan until the pan is full. Pour the chocolate mixture into the pan and let rest for 40 minutes or until all bread is soaked. Bake in a water bath for 40-50 minutes or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean. Let cool overnight.
Sauce: Melt chocolate in the top of a double boiler and transfer to a small saucepan with melted butter, blending well. Add boiling water, sugar, corn syrup, and stir well. Gently boil uncovered for 3 minutes and remove from heat. Add vanilla. To serve, slightly warm the bottom of the cake. Loosen edges of the cake with a knife and invert on a platter. Serve with sauce.
